title: Stop creating legacy journal during initialization while preserving legacy detection
summary: Apply a small v0.4.9 initialization fix so `zed-agentops-init.sh` no longer creates `.agent/journal.jsonl`, while keeping legacy filename knowledge in runtime code for compatibility checks and migration-aware behavior.
outputs: Updated initialization script that does not create `.agent/journal.jsonl`
Retained legacy artifact filename mapping for `journal.jsonl`
Regression coverage for the initialization contract
acceptance_criteria: `zed-agentops-init.sh` does not create `.agent/journal.jsonl` for newly initialized workspaces.
Legacy filename knowledge for `journal.jsonl` remains available in Python code for compatibility and detection purposes.
Initialization-related tests assert the canonical and derived artifact set without requiring creation of the legacy journal file.
The change remains scoped to the small v0.4.9 fix and does not alter canonical transaction artifact behavior.
tasks: Remove the legacy journal creation block from `zed-agentops-init.sh`.
Keep the legacy artifact mapping for `journal.jsonl` in runtime code unchanged unless a narrow adjustment is required.
Update or extend tests so the script contract explicitly excludes creation of `.agent/journal.jsonl` while still recognizing it as a legacy artifact when relevant.
Verify that canonical initialization artifacts remain `.agent/tx_event_log.jsonl` and `.agent/tx_state.json`, with derived artifacts unchanged.
notes: This ticket intentionally avoids using `docs/draft.md`; the user requested a small direct v0.4.9 fix.
Do not remove legacy filename knowledge entirely; only stop generating the file during initialization.
Keep the change minimal and focused on initialization behavior plus any directly related test expectations.
